title: carlina acanthifolia nothosubsp. lecoqii
intro: Carlina acanthifolia nothosubsp. lecoqii, also known as the alpine carline thistle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family (Asteraceae) native to the French Alps. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, promote healing, and protect against oxidative stress. Carlina acanthifolia nothosubsp. lecoqii is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions and injuries.
